description_long: This is a grand, marble-clad bank building inspired by classical Greco-Roman architecture, featuring a prominent portico with six fluted columns supporting a detailed pediment adorned with sculpted reliefs. The roof is steeply pitched and covered with orange terracotta tiles, punctuated by decorative acroteria at the corners and apex, some of which are painted in contrasting colors. The structure sits atop a broad, multi-stepped marble platform, further emphasizing its monumental presence. The side facades are lined with evenly spaced columns, creating a rhythmic colonnade effect, while the front and rear pediments are richly ornamented. The overall appearance is stately and imposing, suggesting a secure and prestigious financial institution. No visible signage, ATMs, or modern security features are present, reinforcing the building's classical aesthetic.
description: A grand, marble bank building with classical columns, ornate pediments, and a tiled roof, evoking Greco-Roman architecture.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with six marble columns, a detailed pediment, and decorative roof ornaments.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long colonnade, orange tiled roof, and absence of visible entrances or signage.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with a similar pediment and column arrangement but no visible entrance details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the colonnade and tiled roof, with architectural symmetry and no entry points.
attribute_entrance_design: Main entrance features a single large doorway framed by six marble columns and topped with an ornate pediment; the door itself is not distinctly visible but is implied to be grand and possibly double-leaf, matching the classical style.
